260101.cherokee: ᎠᏴ ᏧᏓᏏ, ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎠᎩᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏥᎻ ᏦᏍᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᏫᏨᏲᎵᎦ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏥᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᎢᏨᏁᎸᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎦᏴᏎᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ ᎡᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᏥᏯᏅᏛ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏘ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ;
260101.english: Jude, the servant of Jesus Christ, and brother of James, to them that are sanctified by God the Father, and preserved in Jesus Christ, and called:
260101.phonetic: A-yv Tsu-da-si, Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv a-gi-nv-si-da-s-di a-le Tsi-mi tso-s-da-da-nv-tli, wi-tsv-yo-li-ga ni-hi ni-tsi-s-ga-nv-na i-tsv-ne-lv-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi A-ga-yv-se-ge-i, a-le Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv e-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv-hi, a-le e-tsi-ya-nv-dv i-tsa-da-nv-ti i-tsa-li-s-do-di-yi;

260102.cherokee: ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏓᎨᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎡᏥᏁᏉᎡᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ.
260102.english: Mercy unto you, and peace, and love, be multiplied.
260102.phonetic: U-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv a-le nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv a-le a-da-ge-yu-di ge-sv e-tsi-ne-quo-e-di ge-se-s-di.

260103.cherokee: ᎢᏨᎨᏳᎢ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎠᏆᏟᏂᎬᏁᎸ ᏫᏨᏲᏪᎳᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᏗᏳᏉ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎢᎦᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ, ᎤᏚᎸᏗ ᎾᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ ᏫᏨᏲᏪᏔᏁᎲ ᎢᏨᏍᏗᏰᏙᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎢᏣᏗᏒᏎᏗᏱ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏳᎳ ᏗᎨᏥᏲᎯᏎᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ.
260103.english: Beloved, when I gave all diligence to write unto you of the common salvation, it was needful for me to write unto you, and exhort you that ye should earnestly contend for the faith which was once delivered unto the saints.
260103.phonetic: I-tsv-ge-yu-i, i-yu-no a-qua-tli-ni-gv-ne-lv wi-tsv-yo-we-la-ne-di-yi ni-ga-di-yu-quo i-ga-tse-li i-ga-li-s-de-lv-do-di u-gv-wa-li, u-du-lv-di na-qua-li-s-da-ne-lv-gi wi-tsv-yo-we-ta-ne-hv i-tsv-s-di-ye-do-di-yi, na-s-gi u-li-ni-gi-dv i-tsa-di-sv-se-di-yi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv na-s-gi ga-yu-la di-ge-tsi-yo-hi-se-lv-hi tsi-gi u-na-da-nv-ti.

260104.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎤᏂᏴᎸᎯ ᎢᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏗᎨᎪᎥᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᎨᎫᎪᏓᏁᏗᏱ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᏚᎾᏁᎶᏛᎾ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ, ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᎩ ᎾᎵᏏᎾᎯᏍᏛᎾ ᎠᎴᏂᏓᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏯᏅᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏓᏱᎯ ᎤᏩᏒᎯᏳ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ.
260104.english: For there are certain men crept in unawares, who were before of old ordained to this condemnation, ungodly men, turning the grace of our God into lasciviousness, and denying the only Lord God, and our Lord Jesus Christ.
260104.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no i-yu-na-s-di a-ni-s-ga-ya u-de-li-dv u-ni-yv-lv-hi i-gi na-s-gi i-lv-hi-yu nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv di-ge-go-v-hi tsi-gi na-s-gi i-yu-s-di di-ge-gu-go-da-ne-di-yi; U-ne-la-nv-hi ni-du-na-ne-lo-dv-na a-ni-s-ga-ya, gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi a-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-gi na-li-si-na-hi-s-dv-na a-le-ni-da-s-di ge-sv i-ya-nv-ne-hi, a-le a-na-da-yi-hi u-wa-sv-hi-yu U-gv-wi-yu-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi, a-le i-ga-tse-li U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv.

260105.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏆᏚᎵᎭ ᎢᏨᏯᏅᏓᏗᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎦᏳᎳ ᏥᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏚᏭᏓᏓᏎ ᏴᏫ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ ᎦᏓ ᎠᎲ, ᎣᏂ ᏥᏚᏛᏔᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏅᎾ.
260105.english: I will therefore put you in remembrance, though ye once knew this, how that the Lord, having saved the people out of the land of Egypt, afterward destroyed them that believed not.
260105.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-qua-du-li-ha i-tsv-ya-nv-da-di-s-do-di-yi na-s-gi hi-a ga-yu-la tsi-tsi-ga-ta-ha, na-s-gi na U-gv-wi-yu-hi tsi-du-wu-da-da-se yv-wi I-tsi-qui-yi ga-da a-hv, o-ni tsi-du-dv-ta-ne na-s-gi na nu-no-hi-yu-nv-na.

260106.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏗᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏄᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏔᏅᎾ ᏥᎨᏎ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏂᎨᎬᏁᎸ ᏧᏅᏕᏤᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎠᏁᎲᎢ, ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛ ᏗᎬᏩᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏕᎨᎦᎸᏍᏗ ᎤᎵᏏᎬᎢ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎠᏍᏆᎸᎲ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ.
260106.english: And the angels which kept not their first estate, but left their own habitation, he hath reserved in everlasting chains under darkness unto the judgment of the great day.
260106.phonetic: A-le di-ni-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi nu-ni-s-qua-ni-go-ta-nv-na tsi-ge-se nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi ni-ge-gv-ne-lv tsu-nv-de-tse-quo-s-gi-ni u-na-tse-li-go-hi a-ne-hv-i, tsu-da-de-sv-dv di-gv-wa-yo-i-s-di ni-ge-sv-na de-ge-ga-lv-s-di u-li-si-gv-i, ga-lv-quo-di-yu di-gu-go-do-di-yi i-ga a-s-qua-lv-hv gv-di-s-gi.

260107.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏐᏓᎻ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎹᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎾᎥ ᏥᏕᎦᏚᎮᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎮ ᏓᎾᏓᏲᏍᎨ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏗᏂᏏᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᏓᏩᏗᏙᎮ ᎤᏇᏓᎵ ᎠᏍᏓᏳᏛᏍᏗ ᎤᏇᏓᎵ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏓᏕᏯᏔᎲᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᎬᏁᎴᎢ ᎠᏂᎩᎵᏲᎨ ᎤᏂᏍᏛᏗᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏠᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ.
260107.english: Even as Sodom and Gomorrha, and the cities about them in like manner, giving themselves over to fornication, and going after strange flesh, are set forth for an example, suffering the vengeance of eternal fire.
260107.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo So-da-mi a-le Go-ma-li, a-le na-s-gi na na-v tsi-de-ga-du-he-i, na-s-gi-ya na-na-dv-ne-he da-na-da-yo-s-ge u-de-li-dv di-ni-si-di ge-sv tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi, a-le a-ni-s-da-wa-di-do-he u-que-da-li a-s-da-yu-dv-s-di u-que-da-li ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, a-da-de-ya-ta-hv-s-gi ni-ge-gv-ne-le-i a-ni-gi-li-yo-ge u-ni-s-dv-di-s-di ge-sv gv-tlo-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na a-tsi-lv-yi.

260108.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᎠ ᎠᎾᏍᎩᏓᏒᎥᏍᎩ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎾᏅᏁᎭ ᎤᏂᏇᏓᎸᎢ, ᏗᏂᏍᎦᎩ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏐᏢᏗ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ.
260108.english: Likewise also these filthy dreamers defile the flesh, despise dominion, and speak evil of dignities.
260108.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo hi-a a-na-s-gi-da-sv-v-s-gi ga-da-ha na-nv-ne-ha u-ni-que-da-lv-i, di-ni-s-ga-gi nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi, a-le di-gv-wa-ni-so-tlv-di ge-tsi-lv-quo-di.

260109.cherokee: ᏑᏱᎩᎵᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᎾᏗᏒᎸ, ᎤᎾᏗᏒᎸ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎼᏏ ᎠᏰᎸᎢ, ᎹᏱᎩᎵ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏐᏅ ᏳᎳᏫᏎᎴᎢ, ᎯᎠᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏫᏣᏍᎦᎩ.
260109.english: Yet Michael the archangel, when contending with the devil he disputed about the body of Moses, durst not bring against him a railing accusation, but said, The Lord rebuke thee.
260109.phonetic: Su-yi-gi-li-s-gi-ni nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi a-s-gi-na u-na-di-sv-lv, u-na-di-sv-lv a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv Mo-si a-ye-lv-i, ma-yi-gi-li v-tla go-hu-s-di u-so-nv yu-la-wi-se-le-i, hi-a-quo-s-gi-ni nu-we-se-i, U-gv-wi-yu-hi wi-tsa-s-ga-gi.

260110.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏂᎦᏔᎲᎾ; ᎾᏍᎩᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎨᏒ ᏥᎾᎦᏔᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎦᎪᎵᏍᏗᏉ ᏣᏁᎿᎭᎠ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏲ ᎾᎾᏓᏛᏁᎭ.
260110.english: But these speak evil of those things which they know not: but what they know naturally, as brute beasts, in those things they corrupt themselves.
260110.phonetic: Hi-a-s-gi-ni u-so-nv a-ni-no-he-ha na-s-gi na-ni-ga-ta-hv-na; na-s-gi-s-gi-ni u-nv-sv ge-sv tsi-na-ga-ta-ha, na-s-gi-ya ga-go-li-s-di-quo tsa-ne-hna-a a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, na-s-gi na-hna u-yo na-na-da-dv-ne-ha.

260111.cherokee: ᎤᏲ ᏫᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏏ!  ᎨᎾᏰᏃ ᎤᏅᏅ ᎤᏂᏍᏓᏩᏕᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᎦᎫᏴᏓᏁᏗ ᎤᎾᏚᎵᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᎬᎥᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᎨᎮᎾ ᏇᎳᎻ ᎤᎵᏓᏍᏔᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᏛᏔᏅ, ᏚᏂᎦᏘᎸᏒ ᎪᎵ ᏧᎦᏘᎸᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
260111.english: Woe unto them! for they have gone in the way of Cain, and ran greedily after the error of Balaam for reward, and perished in the gainsaying of Core.
260111.phonetic: U-yo wi-nu-na-li-s-da-si!  Ge-na-ye-no u-nv-nv u-ni-s-da-wa-de-nv, a-le ge-ga-gu-yv-da-ne-di u-na-du-li-s-gv u-ni-gv-v-s-gv u-ni-ge-he-na Que-la-mi u-li-da-s-ta-nv, a-le ge-tsi-dv-ta-nv, du-ni-ga-ti-lv-sv Go-li tsu-ga-ti-lv-dv ge-sv-i.

260112.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏲᎯ ᏚᏓᏓᎳ ᏗᏣᏁᎶᏗ ᏕᏣᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᎯᏳ ᎢᏧᎳ ᏕᏣᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᎦ ᎾᏂᎾᏰᏍᎬᎾ ᎠᎾᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᏍᎪᎢ; ᏚᎶᎩᎸ ᎠᎹ ᏂᏚᏁᎲᎾ, ᎤᏃᎴ ᏧᏱᎴᎪᎢ; ᏕᏡᎬ ᏧᎳᏍᏚᏬᎠᏔᎯ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏄᎾᏓᏓᏁᎲᎾ, ᏔᎵ ᎢᏳᏩᎫᏗ ᎤᏩᏴᏒᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ, ᏗᏰᎯᏛ ᏗᎧᎾᏍᏕᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎨᏐᎢ.
260112.english: These are spots in your feasts of charity, when they feast with you, feeding themselves without fear: clouds they are without water, carried about of winds; trees whose fruit withereth, without fruit, twice dead, plucked up by the roots;
260112.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi nv-yo-hi du-da-da-la di-tsa-ne-lo-di de-tsa-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-gv-i, na-hi-yu i-tsu-la-de-tsa-li-s-da-yv-hv-ga na-ni-na-ye-s-gv-na a-na-li-s-da-yv-hv-s-go-i; du-lo-gi-lv a-ma ni-du-ne-hv-na, u-no-le tsu-yi-le-go-i; de-tlu-gv tsu-la-s-du-wo-a-ta-hi, go-hu-s-di nu-na-da-da-ne-hv-na, ta-li i-yu-wa-gu-di u-wa-yv-sv-hi tsi-ge-so-i, di-ye-hi-dv di-ka-na-s-de-tlv-ta-nv-hi tsi-ge-so-i.

260113.cherokee: ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎤᎿᎭᎸᎯ ᏗᎵᏍᏗᎳᏁᎩ, ᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᎾᏕᎰᎯᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎩ; ᏃᏈᏏ ᎤᏂᏅᏅ ᎤᎾᏞᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎬᎿᎭᎨ ᎤᎵᏏᎬ ᏥᎨᎦᏛᏅᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
260113.english: Raging waves of the sea, foaming out their own shame; wandering stars, to whom is reserved the blackness of darkness for ever.
260113.phonetic: A-me-quo-hi u-hna-lv-hi di-li-s-di-la-ne-gi, u-nv-sv u-na-de-ho-hi-s-di-s-gi a-ni-na-nu-go-wi-s-gi; no-qui-si u-ni-nv-nv u-na-tle-dv, na-s-gi u-tsa-ti gv-hna-ge u-li-si-gv tsi-ge-ga-dv-nv-i-s-da-ne-lv u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na.

260114.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎩ, ᎠᏓᏫ ᎤᏕᏅ ᎦᎵᏉᎩᏁ ᏪᎯ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎬ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏁᎢᏍᏔᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏓᎦᎷᏥ ᏓᏘᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎪ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᏧᏙᎵ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ,
260114.english: And Enoch also, the seventh from Adam, prophesied of these, saying, Behold, the Lord cometh with ten thousands of his saints,
260114.phonetic: A-le I-na-gi, A-da-wi u-de-nv ga-li-quo-gi-ne we-hi, na-s-quo a-do-le-ho-s-gv hi-a na-s-gi du-ne-i-s-ta-ne hi-a nu-we-se-i, Gv-ni-yu-quo, U-gv-wi-yu-hi da-ga-lu-tsi da-ti-ne-he-s-di a-s-go i-ya-ga-yv-li tsu-do-li u-na-da-nv-ti,

260115.cherokee: ᏧᏭᎪᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏂᎥᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎬᏍᎪᎸᏗᏱ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᏚᎾᏁᎶᏛᎾ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏣᏘᏂ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᏄᏂᏪᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᏚᎾᏁᎶᏛᎾ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏡᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
260115.english: To execute judgment upon all, and to convince all that are ungodly among them of all their ungodly deeds which they have ungodly committed, and of all their hard speeches which ungodly sinners have spoken against him.
260115.phonetic: Tsu-wu-go-da-ne-di-yi na-ni-v-i, a-le tsu-gv-s-go-lv-di-yi na-ni-v U-ne-la-nv-hi ni-du-na-ne-lo-dv-na nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv ni-ga-v u-tsa-ti-ni di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i, a-le ni-ga-v ni-du-yu-go-dv-na nu-ni-we-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi ni-du-na-ne-lo-dv-na na-s-gi gv-wa-tlu-di-s-gv-i.

260116.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᎪᏁᎶᎯᏌᏘ, ᎤᏁᎵᎯᏍᎩ, ᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᎾᏚᎸᏅᎥᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᏍᏓᏩᏕᎩ; ᏗᏂᎰᎵᏃ ᏓᏅᏗᏍᎪ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᏢᏈᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎪᎢ, ᏴᏫ ᏚᎾᎧᏛ ᏓᏂᎸᏉᏗᏍᎪ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᎬᏩᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
260116.english: These are murmurers, complainers, walking after their own lusts; and their mouth speaketh great swelling words, having men's persons in admiration because of advantage.
260116.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi u-ni-go-ne-lo-hi-sa-ti, u-ne-li-hi-s-gi, u-nv-sv u-na-du-lv-nv-v-s-gv a-ni-s-da-wa-de-gi; di-ni-ho-li-no da-nv-di-s-go u-tsa-ti a-tlv-qui-s-di a-ni-wo-ni-s-go-i, yv-wi du-na-ka-dv da-ni-lv-quo-di-s-go o-s-dv i-gv-wa-na-li-s-da-ne-di u-du-gi u-nv-sv i-yu-s-di.

260117.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ, ᎢᏨᎨᏳᎢ, ᎢᏣᏅᏓᏓ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᏛ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ;
260117.english: But, beloved, remember ye the words which were spoken before of the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ;
260117.phonetic: A-se-no, i-tsv-ge-yu-i, i-tsa-nv-da-da ka-no-he-dv i-lv-hi-yu u-ni-no-he-dv ge-tsi-nv-si-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi i-ga-tse-li Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv tsu-tse-li-ga;

260118.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ, ᎤᎵᏍᏆᎸᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎠᏁᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᎾᏚᎸᏅᎥᏍᎬ ᏂᏚᏳᎪᏛᎾ ᎠᏂᏍᏓᏩᏕᎩ.
260118.english: How that they told you there should be mockers in the last time, who should walk after their own ungodly lusts.
260118.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a tsi-nu-ni-we-sv-gi, u-li-s-qua-lv-di ge-se-s-di a-ne-he-s-di a-ni-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-gi, na-s-gi u-nv-sv u-na-du-lv-nv-v-s-gv ni-du-yu-go-dv-na a-ni-s-da-wa-de-gi.

260119.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᎠᎾᏓᏓᎴᏗᏍᎩ ᏥᎩ, ᎤᏇᏓᎵᏉ ᎠᏂᏍᏓᏩᏕᎩ, ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏄᏁᎲᎾ.
260119.english: These be they who separate themselves, sensual, having not the Spirit.
260119.phonetic: Hi-a na-s-gi na a-na-da-da-le-di-s-gi tsi-gi, u-que-da-li-quo a-ni-s-da-wa-de-gi, A-da-nv-do nu-ne-hv-na.

260120.cherokee: ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎢᏨᎨᏳᎢ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎢᏦᎯᏳᏒ ᎢᏣᎵᎫᏍᏛᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᏓᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎬᏗ ᎢᏣᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ,
260120.english: But ye, beloved, building up yourselves on your most holy faith, praying in the Holy Ghost,
260120.phonetic: Ni-hi-s-gi-ni i-tsv-ge-yu-i, u-tsa-ti ga-lv-quo-di-yu i-tso-hi-yu-sv i-tsa-li-gu-s-dv-di-s-gv-i, Da-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do gv-di i-tsa-do-li-s-di-s-gv-i,

260121.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎡᏥᎨᏳᎢᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏕᏥᏂᏴᏎᏍᏗ, ᎢᏥᎦᏖᏃᎮᏍᏗ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎠᏓᏁᎯ ᏥᎩ.
260121.english: Keep yourselves in the love of God, looking for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ unto eternal life.
260121.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi e-tsi-ge-yu-i-yu ge-sv de-tsi-ni-yv-se-s-di, i-tsi-ga-te-no-he-s-di i-ga-tse-li U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv gv-ni-dv a-da-ne-hi tsi-gi.

260122.english: And of some have compassion, making a difference:

260123.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏐᎢᏃ ᎨᏥᎾᏰᏍᎩ ᏕᏥᏍᏕᎵᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᏕᏨᏕᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏥᎸᏱ; ᎢᏥᏂᏆᏘᎮᏍᏗ ᎠᏄᏬ ᎤᏇᏓᎸ ᎠᏚᏯᏍᏙᏔᏅᎯ.
260123.english: And others save with fear, pulling them out of the fire; hating even the garment spotted by the flesh.
260123.phonetic: A-ni-so-i-no ge-tsi-na-ye-s-gi de-tsi-s-de-li-s-ge-s-di, de-tsv-de-ge-s-di a-tsi-lv-yi; i-tsi-ni-qua-ti-he-s-di a-nu-wo u-que-da-lv a-du-ya-s-do-ta-nv-hi.

260124.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ Ꮎ ᏰᎵᏉ ᎬᏩᏲᏍᏙᏙᏗ ᏥᎩ ᏗᏥᏅᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᎨᏨᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏛᎢ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏁᏥᎳᏫᏎᎲᎾ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᏓᎧᏅ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒᎢ,
260124.english: Now unto him that is able to keep you from falling, and to present you faultless before the presence of his glory with exceeding joy,
260124.phonetic: Hna-quo-no na-s-gi na ye-li-quo gv-wa-yo-s-do-do-di tsi-gi di-tsi-nv-i-s-di-yi, a-le gv-ni-ge-sv i-ge-tsv-ne-di tsi-gi u-tsa-ti u-li-he-li-s-di i-tsa-da-nv-dv-i go-hu-s-di ne-tsi-la-wi-se-hv-na i-gv-yi-di-tlv da-ka-nv ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv-i,

260125.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏒᎯ ᎠᎦᏔᎾᎢ ᏥᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎩᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ, ᎦᎸᏉᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎪᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏍᏆᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.  ᎡᎺᏅ.
260125.english: To the only wise God our Saviour, be glory and majesty, dominion and power, both now and ever. Amen.
260125.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-wa-sv-hi a-ga-ta-na-i tsi-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi I-gi-s-de-li-s-gi, ga-lv-quo-do-di ge-se-s-di, a-le ga-lv-quo-di-yu ge-se-s-di, nu-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi ge-se-s-di, a-le u-li-ni-gi-di-yu ge-se-s-di, go-hi ge-sv a-le u-li-s-qua-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na.  E-me-nv.
